title: narcissus bulbocodium subsp. albidus
intro: Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. albidus is a subspecies of the Narcissus bulbocodium, a type of daffodil plant that is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. albidus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestion, making it a valuable herb for treating a range of ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
